objective: Apply a fork-only runtime compatibility hotfix to remove the CUDA 12.8 host-driver requirement that causes startup failure (`nvidia-container-cli requirement error`) while keeping vLLM behavior stable.
scope: Apply minimal, reversible changes in this fork only to Dockerfile, docker-bake.hcl, docs/runtime-compatibility.md, README.md, and aiwf.request.json. Keep dependency/runtime alignment by matching CUDA base image and PyTorch wheel index build args.
acceptance_criteria: Default container runtime no longer hard-requires CUDA 12.8 and uses CUDA 12.6-compatible base/image index defaults.
Build configuration exposes CUDA runtime and PyTorch wheel index as explicit build args for controlled rollback/override.
Operator docs explicitly state host-driver compatibility and the CUDA 12.4 override path.
Validation gate commands complete: py_compile, .runpod/hub.json JSON load, and docker buildx bake --print.
rollback_plan: Immediate rollback is fork-local git revert of this hotfix commit to restore prior CUDA 12.8 defaults (`CUDA_IMAGE_TAG=12.8.1-base-ubuntu22.04`, `PYTORCH_CUDA_INDEX=cu128`) in Dockerfile/docker-bake. Operational rollback is pinning endpoint image to the last known-good fork tag while revert image is rebuilt and redeployed.
deploy_plan: Deploy fork-only in staged rollout (1->2->4 workers). At each stage verify cold start, successful model load, and one representative inference before scaling.
threat_model: Main risks are binary/runtime mismatch when CUDA base image and wheel index diverge, and performance regressions from runtime downgrade. Mitigation: keep defaults aligned (`12.6.3` + `cu126`), document explicit override pairs, and require staged runtime validation.
